docs.title: Home
docs.text:
self-evolving · end technical debt · code once, live forever AION The Self-Evolving Code Engine.

AI scans your code continuously, automatically rewrites outdated syntax and risky logic, and delivers an evolved codebase every day — keeping your repository perpetually healthy.

Python-first Semgrep + LLM Context-gap reporting CLI and JSON output Read in English 阅读中文文档

docs.title: Choose a language
docs.text:
• English documentation
  • 中文文档

docs.title: Roadmap
docs.text: This roadmap reflects the current shipped functionality in main and the next logical steps for AION.

docs.title: Current status
docs.text:
AION already supports:

  • Python-focused repository and file scanning
  • Repository context extraction via static analysis
  • semgrep --config p/python as the fast first pass
  • Anthropic and OpenAI providers for contextual analysis
  • AI-generated file targeting via markers, Git history, or explicit --ai-generated
  • Rich terminal output and JSON output
  • Deterministic remediation planning for selected high-confidence Python issues
  • Patch artifact generation through aion repair
  • Standalone artifact verification through aion verify
  • A local scan -> repair -> verify incident loop through aion run-incident

docs.title: First autonomy release boundary
docs.text:
The current autonomy release is intentionally conservative.

What it does now:

  • detects incidents
  • plans deterministic remediation
  • generates patch artifacts
  • verifies artifacts locally

What it does not do yet:

  • modify production files in place
  • auto-commit or auto-open pull requests
  • perform repository-wide autonomous remediation workflows
  • support non-Python remediation

docs.title: Deterministic repair coverage today
docs.text:
Current built-in deterministic repair paths cover:

  • raw sqlite f-string queries
  • hardcoded secrets
  • missing auth decorators

docs.title: Phase 0 — Trusted Foundation
docs.text:
• Upgrade scan results from "reports" to structured incident objects.
  • Establish a unified JSON schema so scan → repair → verify stages can be chained reliably.
  • Add fixtures that express the full chain: vulnerability sample → expected patch → expected verification result.
  • Success criterion: the repository can represent a complete incident lifecycle, not just print findings.

docs.title: Phase 1 — Automatic Repair Closed Loop
docs.text:
• Prioritise template-based (deterministic) repairs for well-understood issue classes.
  • Use the LLM only as a fallback when no template covers the pattern.
  • Auto-run after every patch: syntax validation, semgrep re-scan, and the minimal relevant test set.
  • Success criterion: for Python security issues covered by fixtures, AION automatically produces a patch and passes verification end-to-end.

docs.title: Phase 2 — Self-Verification and Learning
docs.text:
• Record every repair attempt: input, patch, verification outcome, and failure reason.
  • Build a repair eval that measures success rate, regression rate, and false-repair rate.
  • Distil rule templates and prompt strategies from failure samples.
  • Success criterion: evaluation covers not only "vulnerability detected" but also "repair correct".

docs.title: Phase 3 — Pre-production Autonomy
docs.text:
• Add an orchestrator prototype that consumes an event queue and drives the scan/repair/verify pipeline.
  • Support staging/sandbox repositories: apply patches automatically and run full verification.
  • Introduce policy gating: configure which issue categories allow automatic repair and which are suggestion-only.
  • Success criterion: the full incident-to-patch-conclusion loop completes without manual step-by-step intervention.

docs.title: Phase 4 — Production Online Self-Healing
docs.text:
• Ingest runtime attack and anomaly signals as incident triggers.
  • Introduce a policy engine with release gates, canary rollout, and automatic rollback.
  • Prioritise hot-fix actions (config / gateway / WAF / feature-flag changes) over in-place code replacement.
  • Success criterion: after an attack is detected, the system autonomously blocks, repairs, verifies, and rolls back — all within defined policy constraints.

docs.title: Guiding principle
docs.text: AION is moving toward self-evolving code, but every phase prioritises safety, determinism, and reviewability over aggressive automation.

docs.title: AION
docs.text:
Code Once, Live Forever.

AION is The Self-Evolving Code Engine — designed to end technical debt and keep your codebase in a perpetual state of health.

Instead of treating every file in isolation, it builds a lightweight profile of the existing repository, uses semgrep as a fast first pass, and only asks the LLM to investigate files that have concrete risk signals or meaningful context gaps.

docs.title: Why this exists
docs.text:
AI-generated code often looks locally reasonable while drifting away from project conventions in ways that increase security risk:

  • Raw sqlite3 usage in a codebase that otherwise standardizes on ORM sessions
  • Missing auth decorators in handlers that should follow an established access pattern
  • Hardcoded secrets where the rest of the repository loads credentials from the environment

AION is designed to catch that mismatch between a generated file and the rest of the repository.

docs.title: Core capabilities
docs.text:
• Scans Python files and repositories from the command line
  • Detects AI-generated files via markers, Git history, or explicit targeting
  • Extracts repository context using static analysis
  • Runs semgrep --config p/python as a fast rule-based pass
  • Uses an LLM to explain security findings in repository context
  • Reports context gaps, fixes, and JSON output for automation

docs.title: Read next
docs.text:
• Installation
  • Usage
  • Configuration
  • How It Works

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Place a .aion.yaml file in the target repository root.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: yaml provider: openai model: gpt-4.1 ignore_paths: - tests/* - scripts/generated_*.py

docs.title: Fields
docs.text: Field Type Description provider string anthropic or openai model string Explicit model name ignore_paths list Glob patterns to skip during scanning

docs.title: Override order
docs.text: CLI flags take precedence over .aion.yaml.

docs.title: Cache
docs.text:
Context extraction results are cached at:

text ~/.aion-context.json

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text: AION is built as a staged pipeline.

docs.title: 1. Identify target files
docs.text:
The CLI resolves Python files from the input path and excludes common directories such as .git, .venv, node_modules, and __pycache__.

It then narrows the scan to likely AI-generated files using:

  • file markers such as Generated by
  • Git history hints such as Copilot or Cursor signatures
  • explicit --ai-generated targets

If no marker is found, it warns and scans all Python files.

docs.title: 2. Extract repository context
docs.text:
The context extractor samples the repository and builds a lightweight profile:

  • imports
  • auth decorators
  • database access patterns
  • function names
  • likely ORM and HTTP client usage

That repository profile is what makes context-gap reporting possible.

docs.title: 3. Run Semgrep first
docs.text: If semgrep is installed, AION runs it as a fast initial pass and collects structured findings from p/python.

docs.title: 4. Escalate to LLM analysis only when needed
docs.text:
The LLM is used when:

  • semgrep found something
  • there are repository-specific fallback signals
  • semgrep is unavailable

This keeps analysis more targeted than sending every file to the model.

docs.title: 5. Report findings
docs.text:
Each finding includes:

  • issue summary
  • severity
  • line number
  • context gap
  • suggested fix

Output is available in rich terminal format or JSON.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.11 or newer
  • uv for environment management
  • Optional: semgrep for the rule-based first pass

docs.title: Local setup
docs.text: bash git clone https://github.com/shenxianpeng/aion.git cd aion uv sync --dev

docs.title: API keys
docs.text:
Choose at least one provider:

bash export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your_key

or

bash export OPENAI_API_KEY=your_key

docs.title: Verify the CLI
docs.text: bash uv run aion --help

docs.title: Scan a repository
docs.text: bash uv run aion scan ./path/to/project

docs.title: Scan known AI-generated files only
docs.text: bash uv run aion scan ./path/to/project \ --ai-generated ./path/to/project/generated_file.py

docs.title: Use OpenAI instead of Anthropic
docs.text: bash uv run aion scan ./path/to/project --provider openai

docs.title: Emit JSON
docs.text: bash uv run aion scan ./path/to/project --output json

docs.title: Verbose mode
docs.text:
bash uv run aion scan ./path/to/project --verbose

Repair and verification flow:

```bash uv run aion repair ./path/to/file.py \ --context-file ./context.json \ --artifact-path ./artifact.json \ --record-path ./repair-record.json

uv run aion verify --artifact-path ./artifact.json

uv run aion run-incident ./path/to/file.py \ --context-file ./context.json \ --record-path ./incident-record.json \ --output json

uv run aion repair-eval ./tests/fixtures \ --records-dir ./repair-records \ --output json

uv run aion process-event ./event.json \ --result-path ./orchestration.json \ --output json

uv run aion process-event-queue ./events.json \ --results-dir ./queue-results \ --output json

uv run aion enqueue-event ./event.json \ --inbox-root ./.aion/inbox

uv run aion list-inbox \ --inbox-root ./.aion/inbox \ --status pending

uv run aion process-inbox \ --inbox-root ./.aion/inbox \ --output json

uv run aion serve-webhook \ --inbox-root ./.aion/inbox \ --host 127.0.0.1 \ --port 8080

uv run aion create-release-candidate ./.aion/inbox/results/.json \ --releases-root ./.aion/releases

uv run aion approve-release \ --approver alice \ --releases-root ./.aion/releases

uv run aion advance-release \ --releases-root ./.aion/releases

uv run aion rollback-release \ --reason "failed canary metrics" \ --releases-root ./.aion/releases

uv run aion plan-defense ./.aion/inbox/results/.json \ --output json ```

The current autonomy release generates patch artifacts and verifies them locally. It does not rewrite production files in place. repair-eval runs the deterministic repair pipeline across fixture cases and reports repair success rate, verification pass rate, false-fix rate, and rollback rate. process-event is the staged orchestration entrypoint. It accepts an event payload, applies policy gating, and only runs approved remediations inside a sandbox workspace. process-event-queue accepts a JSON array of events and reports queue-level metrics while persisting one result file per event. The inbox commands provide a persistent event queue under .aion/inbox, so runtime alerts can be enqueued and processed incrementally. serve-webhook exposes POST /events and stores accepted events in the inbox for asynchronous processing. The release commands persist staged rollout candidates under .aion/releases and support approval, phased advancement, rejection, and rollback. plan-defense renders runtime containment actions that should be applied before or alongside code rollout, such as gateway blocks, WAF rules, feature flags, and dependency pins.

Example orchestration settings in .aion.yaml:

yaml auto_repair_issue_types: - raw_sqlite_query - hardcoded_secret auto_repair_min_confidence: 0.90 sandbox_mode: repository sandbox_verification_commands: - python -m pytest tests/unit auto_approve_verified_fixes: false rollback_on_verification_failure: true

When sandbox verification commands are configured, AION runs them inside the staged workspace and records each command's exit code, stdout/stderr, and a rollout recommendation: - approved_for_rollout when sandbox verification passes and auto approval is enabled - rollback when verification fails and rollback-on-failure is enabled - needs_human_review otherwise

Verbose mode prints the extracted context profile, Semgrep findings, fallback reasons, and token estimates to stderr.

docs.title: Typical workflow
docs.text:
1. Point the tool at a repository or a generated file.
  2. Let it identify candidate Python files.
  3. Review warnings about AI-generated detection or missing semgrep.
  4. Inspect context-aware findings and suggested fixes.

docs.title: AION
docs.text:
AION 是自进化代码引擎——终结技术债，让代码库进入永生状态。

Code Once, Live Forever. 一次编写，永远鲜活。

它不是把每个文件孤立地看待，而是先从现有仓库中提取轻量级上下文，再用 semgrep 做第一轮规则扫描，只有在存在明确风险信号或明显上下文偏差时， 才交给 LLM 做进一步解释和判断。

docs.title: 这个项目解决什么问题
docs.text:
AI 生成代码经常“局部看起来合理”，但会悄悄偏离项目原有约定，从而引入安全风险：

  • 整个项目都用 ORM，会话管理也统一，但新文件直接写了 sqlite3
  • 大部分接口都有鉴权 decorator，新增 handler 却漏掉了
  • 项目原本从环境变量读取密钥，生成代码却把密钥硬编码进文件

AION 要抓的就是这种“生成代码”和“项目上下文”之间的错位。

docs.title: 核心能力
docs.text:
• 在命令行扫描 Python 文件或整个仓库
  • 通过文件标记、Git 历史或显式参数识别 AI 生成文件
  • 基于静态分析提取项目上下文
  • 先运行 semgrep --config p/python
  • 再用 LLM 在仓库上下文里解释安全问题
  • 输出 context gap、修复建议和 JSON 结果

docs.title: 下一步阅读
docs.text:
• 安装
  • 使用
  • 配置
  • 工作原理

docs.title: 配置
docs.text: 在目标仓库根目录创建 .aion.yaml。

docs.title: 示例
docs.text: yaml provider: openai model: gpt-4.1 ignore_paths: - tests/* - scripts/generated_*.py

docs.title: 字段说明
docs.text: 字段 类型 说明 provider string anthropic 或 openai model string 显式指定模型名 ignore_paths list 扫描时忽略的 glob 模式

docs.title: 覆盖优先级
docs.text: 命令行参数优先级高于 .aion.yaml。

docs.title: 缓存位置
docs.text:
上下文提取缓存保存在：

text ~/.aion-context.json

docs.title: 工作原理
docs.text: AION 是一个分阶段执行的自进化分析流程。

docs.title: 1. 识别目标文件
docs.text:
CLI 会从输入路径中解析 Python 文件，并自动排除 .git、.venv、 node_modules、__pycache__ 等常见目录。

然后再通过以下方式缩小到更可能是 AI 生成的文件：

  • 文件内容里的生成标记，例如 Generated by
  • Git 历史中的 Copilot / Cursor 迹象
  • --ai-generated 显式指定的文件或目录

如果没有发现任何标记，它会给出 warning，然后扫描全部 Python 文件。

docs.title: 2. 提取仓库上下文
docs.text:
上下文提取器会基于静态分析构建一个轻量级仓库画像，包括：

  • imports
  • 鉴权 decorators
  • 数据库访问模式
  • 函数名
  • 常见 ORM 和 HTTP client

这一步决定了后续能不能给出有价值的 context gap 判断。

docs.title: 3. 先跑 Semgrep
docs.text: 如果系统中安装了 semgrep，AION 会先运行 p/python 规则集， 把它作为低成本的第一轮筛查。

docs.title: 4. 只在必要时升级到 LLM
docs.text:
只有在以下情况，才会调用 LLM：

  • semgrep 命中了问题
  • 存在仓库上下文上的 fallback 风险信号
  • 本地没有安装 semgrep

这样可以比“所有文件都发给模型”更聚焦，也更便宜。

docs.title: 5. 输出结果
docs.text:
每条问题都会尽量包含：

  • 问题描述
  • 严重级别
  • 行号
  • context gap
  • 修复建议

结果支持富文本终端输出，也支持 JSON 输出。

docs.title: 环境要求
docs.text:
• Python 3.11 及以上
  • 使用 uv 管理环境
  • 可选安装 semgrep，用于第一轮规则扫描

docs.title: 本地安装
docs.text: bash git clone https://github.com/shenxianpeng/aion.git cd aion uv sync --dev

docs.title: 配置 API Key
docs.text:
至少配置一个模型提供方：

bash export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your_key

或者：

bash export OPENAI_API_KEY=your_key

docs.title: 验证命令
docs.text: bash uv run aion --help

docs.title: 扫描整个仓库
docs.text: bash uv run aion scan ./path/to/project

docs.title: 只扫描已知的 AI 生成文件
docs.text: bash uv run aion scan ./path/to/project \ --ai-generated ./path/to/project/generated_file.py

docs.title: 切换到 OpenAI
docs.text: bash uv run aion scan ./path/to/project --provider openai

docs.title: 输出 JSON
docs.text: bash uv run aion scan ./path/to/project --output json

docs.title: 输出详细调试信息
docs.text:
bash uv run aion scan ./path/to/project --verbose

修复与验证闭环：

```bash uv run aion repair ./path/to/file.py \ --context-file ./context.json \ --artifact-path ./artifact.json \ --record-path ./repair-record.json

uv run aion verify --artifact-path ./artifact.json

uv run aion run-incident ./path/to/file.py \ --context-file ./context.json \ --record-path ./incident-record.json \ --output json

uv run aion repair-eval ./tests/fixtures \ --records-dir ./repair-records \ --output json

uv run aion process-event ./event.json \ --result-path ./orchestration.json \ --output json

uv run aion process-event-queue ./events.json \ --results-dir ./queue-results \ --output json

uv run aion enqueue-event ./event.json \ --inbox-root ./.aion/inbox

uv run aion list-inbox \ --inbox-root ./.aion/inbox \ --status pending

uv run aion process-inbox \ --inbox-root ./.aion/inbox \ --output json

uv run aion serve-webhook \ --inbox-root ./.aion/inbox \ --host 127.0.0.1 \ --port 8080

uv run aion create-release-candidate ./.aion/inbox/results/.json \ --releases-root ./.aion/releases

uv run aion approve-release \ --approver alice \ --releases-root ./.aion/releases

uv run aion advance-release \ --releases-root ./.aion/releases

uv run aion rollback-release \ --reason "failed canary metrics" \ --releases-root ./.aion/releases

uv run aion plan-defense ./.aion/inbox/results/.json \ --output json ```

当前首版自治能力只生成补丁 artifact 并在本地验证，不会直接原地改写生产文件。 repair-eval 会批量运行确定性修复流水线，并输出修复成功率、验证通过率、误修率和回滚率。 process-event 是当前控制平面原型入口：它接收事件 payload，做策略门控，并只在 sandbox 工作区里执行获批修复。 process-event-queue 接收一个事件数组，批量执行编排，并为每个事件落盘结果，同时输出队列级指标。 inbox 命令提供了持久化事件队列 .aion/inbox，让运行时告警可以先入队，再按批次增量处理。 serve-webhook 会暴露 POST /events，把收到的事件直接写入 inbox，供后续异步编排处理。 release 命令会在 .aion/releases 下保存 staged rollout candidate，并支持批准、分阶段推进、拒绝和回滚。 plan-defense 会输出运行时优先的防护动作，比如 gateway block、WAF rule、feature flag、dependency pin，以及后续 code patch。

.aion.yaml 里的编排配置示例：

yaml auto_repair_issue_types: - raw_sqlite_query - hardcoded_secret auto_repair_min_confidence: 0.90 sandbox_mode: repository sandbox_verification_commands: - python -m pytest tests/unit auto_approve_verified_fixes: false rollback_on_verification_failure: true

配置了 sandbox_verification_commands 后，AION 会在 staged workspace 内执行这些项目级验证命令，并记录每条命令的退出码、stdout/stderr，以及最终发布建议： - approved_for_rollout：sandbox 验证通过且启用了自动批准 - rollback：验证失败且启用了失败即回滚 - needs_human_review：其余情况

--verbose 会把上下文提取结果、Semgrep 结果、fallback 原因和 token 估算输出到 stderr。

docs.title: 推荐使用流程
docs.text:
1. 指向一个仓库或一个已生成文件。
  2. 让工具自动识别候选 Python 文件。
  3. 查看 AI 文件识别和 semgrep 可用性的 warning。
  4. 重点阅读 context-aware 的问题解释和修复建议。
